result:
Implemented support for custom note templates loaded from `notes/templates/*.md`.

In [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go), `create --template` and `template <name>` now resolve against a merged template registry: the existing built-ins plus disk-backed templates from `notes/templates`. Custom templates reuse the normal note format, so `# Title` becomes the default title, `Tags:` become default tags, and the remaining body becomes the template body. `template` listing now includes both built-in and custom templates, and CLI help text reflects that.

In [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go), I added coverage for creating from a custom template, creating with an explicit title via `template <name>`, and listing custom templates. I also updated [README.md](/home/changkun/null-codex/README.md) to document the new `notes/templates/<name>.md` convention.

I could not run `go test ./...` because `go` is not installed in this environment (`/bin/bash: go: command not found`).
